description: Ground becomes 'thepalu' through sustained tending for food, habitat, medicine, or pleasure. One household may tend it, or a community may share the work. 'kosha' (field) is the wider open area; size alone does not make a garden.
articulatory_notes: Unvoiced 'th' starts with the tongue lightly touching the upper teeth before front 'e'. The lips meet and release into stressed open 'pa'; lateral 'l' at the alveolar ridge carries the word into high-back rounded 'u'.
usage_notes: Possessors and communities come first in 'mia thepalu' (my garden) and 'sila thepalu' (the community's garden). 'temoe' (tend) and 'sorila' (cultivate) describe the work done there; the surrounding account tells who chose it and who benefits.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A shared garden can join food, habitat, beauty, and common work in one place. It still has doors: who enters, who works, and who decides.

semantic_domains.community: ground that may be tended and used in common
semantic_domains.nature: tended ground where plants and other life are deliberately supported
semantic_domains.spatial: a place identified by ongoing cultivation or care
